methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $12M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Housing & Construction (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 1 same-sector peers exist in Housing & Construction, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
BotBuilt is a seed-stage company in Housing & Construction founded by Barrett Ames and Colin Devine. The company has raised $12.4M to date. The relevant comp universe in this sector is sparse: three private peers (ICON, Apis Cor, and American Housing Corporation) exist, with no public comparables available. Critically, no same-stage or adjacent-stage peers with similar valuation tiers could be identified in sufficient density to support a peer-median analysis.

Given data constraints, the valuation relies on a stage-progression methodology. Using total raised ($12.4M) as a proxy for current implied value and applying a 2.5x multiplier typical of Seed-to-Series A progression yields $31M. This method assumes BotBuilt's progression follows standard venture funnel dynamics and that raised capital reasonably approximates the floor of founder + early investor conviction.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $26M to $36M, with a median of $31M. The narrow methodological foundation—one applicable technique rather than true triangulation—is a material limitation. Key uncertainties include the strength of BotBuilt's unit economics relative to the three identified peers, the durability of Housing & Construction venture economics post-2023, and whether the 2.5x step-up multiplier accurately reflects this subsector's typical progression velocity. Additional financial metrics (revenue run rate, customer concentration, burn multiple) would strengthen conviction in this range.
